原文: Friendship needs care① and attention② to keep it in good health③. Here are five ways to sustain④(保持) long-distance⑤ friendships.

原文: Long-lasting⑦ friendships share the characteristic⑧ that both sides equally⑨ contact⑩(联系) and share with one another. With busy schedules⑪, squeezing⑫ in phone calls can be a challenge⑬. D. Try to find a time that works for both of you and stick⑭ to it.

原文: Make sure you have communicated⑯ with your friend about how frequently⑰ each of you wants to be contacted and what method⑱ works best for you both. E. Friends need to talk about their preferred⑲ methods of communication. There are alternatives⑳ to constant㉑ written communication, such as leaving voice messages or having a group chat.
翻译: 确保你已经和朋友沟通过，你们各自希望多久联系一次，以及哪种方法对双方都最有效。E. 朋友需要谈论他们偏好的沟通方式。除了持续的书面沟通，还有其他选择，比如留语音信息或进行群聊。

原文: G. You may be the friend who left or the one who was left behind㉓. The friend who is remaining㉔ needs to be sensitive㉕ to all the additional㉖ time demands placed on the friend who has moved. The one in the new environment㉗ should be sympathetic㉘ to the fact that your friend may feel abandoned㉙.
翻译: G. 你可能是离开的朋友，也可能是被留下的朋友。留下的朋友需要对搬走的朋友所面临的所有额外时间需求保持敏感。身处新环境的朋友应该体谅你的朋友可能感到被抛弃的事实。

原文: Anniversaries㉚ and birthdays carry even more weight㉛ in long-distance friendships. Although technology㉜ might make day-to-day communication possible, extra effort㉝ goes a long way on special days. Simply keeping a diary㉞ that keeps track㉟ of friends' birthdays and other important dates will make sure nothing slips㊱ by you.
翻译: 在远距离友谊中，周年纪念日和生日甚至更加重要。尽管科技可能使日常沟通成为可能，但在特殊日子里额外的努力会大有帮助。简单地记日记来记录朋友的生日和其他重要日期，将确保你不会错过任何事。

原文: F. It is easy to have a sense㊳ of connectedness㊴ through social media, but long-distance friendships—even close ones—may require more conscious㊵ effort to sustain. Try to seek㊶ out chances to renew㊷ friendships. How to do it? Just spend face-to-face㊸ time together whenever possible.
翻译: F. 通过社交媒体很容易产生一种连接感，但远距离友谊——即使是亲密的友谊——可能需要更多有意识的努力来维持。试着寻找机会来重续友谊。如何做到呢？只要可能，就花时间面对面相处。
